Description.Name: The Reward
Description.Details:
<color=#BFBFBF>The shuttle arrives exactly when Arelle said it would. No transponder, no hail, no pilot visible through the canopy. It docks, offloads three crates onto the cargo bay floor, and detaches before Sumire can even log the approach vector.</color>

<color=#BFBFBF>Yang is already in the bay with a pry bar before the docking clamps finish cycling.</color>

<color=#BFBFBF>He cracks the first crate and goes still.</color>

"That's an XL engine. Clan-spec. The heat shielding alone on this thing is worth more than most of our mechs."

<color=#BFBFBF>He runs a hand along the housing, checking the seams.</color> "No production stamps. No serial numbers. Whoever built this didn't want it traced. But the machining is clean. This is front-line hardware."

<color=#BFBFBF>The second and third crates open easier. Packed in shock-dampened cradles, four ER PPCs sit in matched pairs, barrel shrouds still sealed in factory wrap.</color>

"Clan extended range PPCs, four of them."

<color=#BFBFBF>Yang steps back and looks at the open crates, hands on his hips.</color>

"I'll say this for the Star Colonel. She knows how to make an introduction."

<color=#BFBFBF>He reaches into the third crate and pulls out a sealed envelope tucked against the packing foam. Plain paper. Handwritten. He reads it once, then holds it up.</color>

"There's a note. Signed by Jorgensson herself."

<color=#DFDFDF>"Commander. These are yours regardless of what follows. But understand this clearly. If you entertain the thought of playing both sides, I will know before you have finished thinking it. Do not test me on this.

—Star Colonel Arelle Jorgensson"</color>

<color=#BFBFBF>Darius appears at the bay entrance, arms crossed.</color> "So that's what good faith looks like from the Watch. Clan-grade weapons and a power plant that could run a small city."

<color=#BFBFBF>Yang hands him the note. Darius reads it and exhales slowly.</color>

"Gifts and threats in the same crate. At least she's efficient."

<color=#BFBFBF>Yang shrugs.</color> "Could be worse."

Description.Name: Prepare to leave for Butzfleth
Description.Details: Yang wheels the last crate toward the mech bay, whistling something tuneless. The bay falls quiet. Darius doesn't leave. He stands by the door, holding a datapad. He doesn't say anything. He just hands it over. The message is only three words. The sender ID is one you've seen before. Mizuki. "You are dead." Darius watches your face. "Came in about ten minutes ago. Tight-beam. No encryption this time. She wanted us to know she wasn't hiding it." You hand the datapad back. Darius tucks it under his arm. "So. Three words. Short and sweet. You think she's saving the longer version for when she finds us?" You don't answer. Darius fills the silence himself. "Yeah, me neither. At least Arelle sends engine parts with her threats." [A new flashpoint has been created in the Butzfleth system for the continuation of the campaign]
